_note: Starter mnemonics for Spanish vocab. Keyed by entry text. Never overwrites user-written mnemonics.
hola: 'Hola' sounds like 'holla' — you holler hello across the street. Short, punchy, friendly.
gracias: Sounds like 'gra-see-as'. 'Gracias' — imagine saying 'Grass? Yes!' and thanking someone for their lovely lawn.
manzana: Sounds like 'man-ZAH-nah'. Imagine a man named Zana who sells apples 🍎. Man-zan-a = manzana.
agua: Sounds like 'AH-gwah'. Imagine an iguana drinking water — 'ah, gwa!' Also close to 'aqua' (water in Latin).
libro: Sounds like 'LEE-bro'. 'Library bro' — your bro at the library reading a book 📚.
gato: Sounds like 'GAH-toh'. 'Got-oh no, the cat scratched me!' Quick association: cat = gato.
perro: Rolls the R: 'PEH-rroh'. Imagine a dog saying 'Perrro!' with a growl. The rolled R sounds like a dog's rumble.
comer: Sounds like 'co-MEHR'. 'Come here to eat!' — come + mer(ge) at the dinner table. Comer = to eat.
beber: Sounds like 'beh-BEHR'. Baby talk for drinking — 'beh-beh' like a baby wanting its bottle. To drink = beber.
grande: Almost identical to English 'grand' — a grand (grande) size. Grande from your coffee order! Big.
pequeño: Sounds like 'peh-KEN-yo'. 'Peh-Kenny-oh' — imagine tiny Kenny, the small one of the group.
bueno: Sounds like 'BWEH-noh'. 'Buenos dias!' — you already know this. Bueno = good. Buen = good prefix.
persona: Nearly identical to English 'person' — persona, person. A persona is who a person presents to the world.
amigo: Familiar from English — 'amigo' is used in English too! Friend, buddy, pal. Easy win.
estudiante: Shares root with 'student' — estudiante is just the Spanish elaboration. Estudi-ante = one who studies.
profesor: Almost identical to English 'professor' — profesor. The accent shifts but the word is the same.
trabajar: Sounds like 'trah-bah-HAR'. 'Trouble-har' — working is trouble! Trabajar = to work.
estudiar: Shares root with 'study' — estudi-ar. The -ar ending marks it as an infinitive verb. To study.
gustar: Sounds like 'goos-TAR'. Means 'to please/to be pleasing' — 'me gusta' = it pleases me = I like it. Gust = taste.
bonito: Sounds like 'boh-NEE-toh'. 'Boh-neat-oh' — something neat and tidy is beautiful. Also a type of tuna fish!
feliz: Sounds like 'feh-LEES'. 'Feliz Navidad!' — you know this from the Christmas song. Felix = happy in Latin.
rápido: Sounds like 'RAH-pee-doh'. Shares root with 'rapid' — rápido = rapid = fast. The accent marks the stress.
lento: Sounds like 'LEN-toh'. Musical term — 'lento' in music means slow tempo. Same in Spanish.
comprar: Sounds like 'cohm-PRAHR'. 'Com-prar' — compare prices before you comprar (buy). Shopping = comparing.
dinero: Sounds like 'dee-NEH-roh'. 'Dee-nero' — Robert De Niro has lots of dinero (money) 💰.
